questionHtml:
The dotted line represents the volume of carbon dioxide evolved when excess calcium carbonate is added to hydrochloric acid.

Which graph represents the production of carbon dioxide when excess calcium carbonate is added to the same volume of hydrochloric acid of double concentration?

meta.title: The dotted line represents the volume of carbon dioxide evolved when excess calcium carbonate is added to hydrochloric acid. Which graph represents the production of carbon dioxide
breadcrumbLabels: Reactivity 2. How much, how fast and how far?
Reactivity 2.2—How fast? The rate of chemical change
Reactivity 2.2.1—The rate of reaction is expressed as the change in concentration of a particular reactant/product per unit time.
